title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 379.978 - Written disaster plan, insurer to develop, contents
text:
379.978.  Written disaster plan, insurer to develop, contents. — Every insurance company which insures property for loss caused by earthquake, whether by policy, endorsement, rider or otherwise, shall prepare and retain a written disaster plan covering earthquakes.  This plan shall include specific provisions regarding procedures for handling claims under the insurance company's issued policies or endorsements covering loss or damage from the peril of earthquake.
(L. 1992 H.B. 1574 § 16)
